KPI Indicators — EASA Audit Compliance

Compliance performance indicators (KPIs) required by EASA in accordance with Regulation (EU) 965/2012, ORO.GEN.200, ORO.AOC.130 and ORO.GEN.150. These indicators measure the quality and effectiveness of the internal audit programme and the handling of regulatory findings.

EASA ref.: ORO.AOC.130 EASA ref.: ORO.GEN.150 EASA ref.: AMC1 ORO.GEN.200
— Calculated from actual audit grid data
📊 Key Performance Indicators (KPI) — Audit Compliance
Overall compliance rate
% findings without critical finding
—
—
EASA target : ≥ 90% (ideal ≥ 95%)
⊘ Loading data…
Open critical findings
Unclosed Level 1 findings
—
—
EASA target : 0 (zero tolerance — L1)
⊘ Loading data…
CAP closure rate
% action plans closed within deadlines
—
—
EASA target : ≥ 85% (ideal ≥ 90%)
⊘ Loading data…
Audits completed / planned
% of programme completed
—
—
EASA target : 100% over the cycle (≤ 24 months)
⊘ Loading data…
Average CAP response time
Working days (manual entry)
—
—
L1 ≤ 7d · critical ≤ 14d · major ≤ 30d
⊘ Enter value below
0j14j ✓ critical30j ⚠ major90j
Repeat findings
% findings occurring 2+ times
—
—
EASA target : ≤ 5% (alert > 10%)
⊘ Loading data…
📋 KPI Dashboard — Regulatory Compliance
KPI ref. Indicator EASA category Current value Alert threshold Target Trend Status EASA reference
KPI-01 Overall audit compliance rate Regulatory compliance 87% < 80% ≥ 90% → stable Watch ORO.AOC.130
KPI-02 Unclosed Level 1 findings Critical compliance 2 findings > 0 0 ↑ +2 ALERT ORO.GEN.150 / GM1
KPI-03 CAP closure rate within deadlines Corrective actions 76% < 60% ≥ 85% ↑ +4% Watch ORO.GEN.150
KPI-04 Repeat findings (recurrence) Corrective effectiveness 15% > 10% ≤ 5% ↑ +5% ALERT AMC1 ORO.GEN.200
KPI-05 Audits completed vs planned Programme execution 100% < 90% 100% → stable OK ORO.AOC.130(a)
KPI-06 Average CAP response time (critical) Corrective responsiveness 18j > 14j ≤ 14j ↑ +4j Watch GM1 ORO.GEN.150
Safety Review Board (SRB)
Periodic safety & compliance review — Analysis of KPIs/SPIs, safety occurrences, corrective actions, trends and operational risk control.
1
Participants & attendance
ORO.GEN.200(a) · AMC1 ORO.AOC.130(a)(1)
▼

List of SRB participants with attendance, excused absence and possible substitute. Guests may be added with their function and name.

Function Last name / First name Present Excused Substitute Action
Accountable Manager (SRB Chair)
Safety Manager (SM)
Nameinated Person Flight Operation (NPFO)
Nameinated Person Crew Training (NPCT)
Nameinated Person Ground Operation (NPGO)
Nameinated Person Continuing Airworthiness (NPCA)
Compliance Monitoring Manager (CMM)
2
KPI / SPI indicator review
ORO.GEN.200(a)(3) · AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(3) · GM1 ORO.AOC.130(a)
▼

EASA requires an analysis of SPI trends, alert thresholds and targets. Each indicator in alert must be discussed and decided upon during the SRB.

3
Audit programme results
ORO.AOC.130(a) · GM1 ORO.AOC.130(a)(2)(i)
▼

Summary presentation of the results of all audits completed since the last SRB. Each Level 1 finding must be discussed individually.

4
Analysis of safety occurrences & events
Reg. (UE) 376/2014 · ORO.GEN.200(a)(4) · AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
▼

Review of all reported occurrences (MOR, voluntary ASRs, AIRPROX, incidents techniques). Root-cause analysis and actions decided.

5
Operational risk management review
ORO.GEN.200(a)(3) · AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
▼

Review of identified risks, rating, control measures and barrier effectiveness. EASA requiert a risk matrix documented and periodic reviewment.

Safety Risk Severity
Catastrophic Hazardous Major Minor Negligible
Probability A B C D E
Frequent 5 5A 5B 5C 5D 5E
Occasional 4 4A 4B 4C 4D 4E
Remote 3 3A 3B 3C 3D 3E
Improbable 2 2A 2B 2C 2D 2E
Extremely improbable 1 1A 1B 1C 1D 1E
No level selected
6
Safety Management System (SMS) Review
ORO.GEN.200 · AMC1 ORO.GEN.200 · Annex II Reg. (EU) 965/2012
▼

Assessment of overall SMS effectiveness. EASA requires the SRB to assess the four SMS components: policy/objectives, risk management, assurance and safety promotion.

7
Follow-up of previous SRB actions
AMC1 ORO.AOC.130(a)(2)(ii)
▼

Systematic review of the status of all actions decided during the previous SRB. Any action not closed within the deadlines must be justified and assigned a new deadline.

8
New SRB decisions & action plan
ORO.GEN.200(a)(5) · AMC1 ORO.AOC.130(a)(2)(iii)
▼

Formal recording of all decisions made during the SRB. Each decision must have a designated owner, a deadline and a verification indicator.

9
Regulatory compliance & change management
ORO.GEN.130 · AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(7)
▼

Review of EASA / DGAC regulatory developments affecting the organization. Change management is an explicit SMS requirement (ORO.GEN.130).

10
Approval & signatures
ORO.GEN.200(a) · AMC1 ORO.AOC.130
▼

The SRB minutes must be signed by the Accountable Manager and archived. EASA may request access to these records during an inspection.

📈 CMS Dashboard — EASA Indicators (ORO.GEN.200 / AMC1)

Dashboard of compliance performance indicators (KPIs) and safety objectives expected by EASA for a Part-ORO-compliant Compliance Monitoring System (CMS). Reference: AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(3), AMC2 ORO.GEN.200, GM1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6).

🎯 Key performance indicators (KPI) — Summary view
—
Overall compliance rate
EASA objective : ≥ 90 %
—
Open Level 1 findings
Objective: 0 by D+30
—
Open Level 2 findings
Objective: 0 by D+90
—
Audits completed / planned
Objective: 100 % of the plan annuel
—
Corrective action closure rate
Objective : ≥ 85 % within deadlines
—
Finding recurrence rate
Objective : < 10 %
—
Voluntary reports (ASR/SFRMS)
Objective : increasing trend / year
—
CMS/SMS training coverage
Objective: 100% relevant personnel
📋 EASA CMS objectives by domain (AMC1 ORO.GEN.200)
🏛Organization & ResponsibilitiesORO.GEN.200(a)(1)
  • Accountable manager appointed and accessible
  • Lines of responsibility clearly defined and documented
  • Key positions filled with identified deputies
  • Compliance manager independence guaranteed
  • Direct CM access to the accountable manager
🔒Safety policy & Just CultureORO.GEN.200(a)(2)
  • Safety policy signed by the accountable manager
  • Just culture documented and applied
  • Measurable safety objectives defined annually
  • Policy communicated to all personnel
  • Annual review of the safety policy
⚠️Hazard identification & risk managementORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
  • Formal hazard identification process (HAZID)
  • Risk matrix approved and used
  • Risk register kept up to date
  • Mitigation measures tracked and verified
  • Systematic feedback loop (REX/SFRMS)
👥Competency management & trainingORO.GEN.200(a)(4)
  • Initial CMS training for all CM personnel
  • Recurrent SMS training every 24 months
  • Training records retained ≥ 5 years
  • Competency assessment documented
  • Specific internal audit training for auditors
📄Documentation & document controlORO.GEN.200(a)(5)
  • CMS manual approved and current
  • Internal audit procedures documented
  • Current document list kept up to date
  • Document review process defined
  • Audit reports archived ≥ 5 years
📊Performance monitoring & continuous improvementORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
  • Compliance KPIs defined and measured quarterly
  • Findings follow-up dashboard updated
  • Annual trend analysis formalized
  • Annual audit programme approved
  • Management review (SRB) at least once per year
🔗Change managementORO.GEN.200(a)(7)
  • Change management process documented
  • Risk assessment before any significant change
  • Notification to the competent authority if required
  • Post-change review formalized
🤝Contracting & CAMO/145 interfaceORO.GEN.205
  • List of contractors subject to audit kept up to date
  • Contracts defining compliance responsibilities
  • Contractor audits planned and completed
  • Interface with CAMO and Part-145 documented
📉 CMS objective follow-up by domain
🚨 EASA alerts & points of attention
⚠️
Level 1 closure deadline : Every Level 1 finding must receive immediate corrective action and be closed within 30 days. After this deadline, the competent authority must be notified (ORO.GEN.130).
🔴
Mandatory annual SRB : The Safety Review Board must be held at least once per year with the accountable manager. The minutes must be retained and made available to the authority (AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)).
✅
Voluntary report (SFRMS) : An increasing number of voluntary reports compared with the previous year is a positive safety culture indicator. The EASA objective is an annual upward trend.
📋
Audit programme : The programme must cover all activities within a maximum of 12 to 24 months. High-risk areas must be audited more frequently (AMC2 ORO.GEN.200).
📈 Quarterly trend table
IndicatorT1T2T3T4ObjectiveTrend
Overall compliance rate ≥ 90 % —
Cumulative Level 1 findings 0 —
Cumulative Level 2 findings Decreasing —
On-time closure rate ≥ 85 % —
Voluntary reports (ASR) Increasing —
Audits completed vs planned 100 % —
Finding recurrence rate < 10 % —
🛡
Safety Review Board (SRB) — Complete Checklist
All points required by EASA for a compliant SRB · ORO.GEN.200 · AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) · GM1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)

📌 General SRB intraining

Total points
0
Addresseds
0
In progress

📋 Review of the audit programme for the past year

▼
Presentation of the programme d'audit annuel
Summary of planned vs completed audits, execution rate and reasons for any schedule changes
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
Summary of findings and observations
Namebre total de findings by level (Level 1, Level 2, observations), breakdown by regulatory domain
ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
Corrective action closure status
CAP follow-up (Corrective Action Plans) : closed, in cours, overdue. Identification of the recurring actions
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
Analysis of corrective action effectiveness
Verification that corrective actions have eliminated root causes. Finding recurrence rate
ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
Contractor audit review
Results of audits completed chez the contractors and external providers (CAMO, MRO, handling, etc.)
ORO.GEN.205
2

📊 Safety performance & indicators (SPIs / KPIs)

▼
Safety Performance Indicator review (SPI)
Analysis of each defined SPI: current value vs target, 12-month trend and explanation of deviations
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
Safety report analysis (ASR / SFRMS)
Volume of voluntary reports, categorization, top 5 of the themes, Y-1 comparison, feedback to reporters
ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
Review of safety events & incidents
Incidents notified to the authority, internal investigations conducted, lessons learned and preventive measures taken
ORO.GEN.160 / Reg. EU 376/2014
Trend analysis & monitoring data
Data FDM/FOQA (if applicable), LOSA trends, recurring exceedances, fatigue crew reporting
AMC2 ORO.GEN.200
Comparison with sector data (benchmarking)
Positioning vs EASA, IATA and IOSA data, and external safety bulletins relevant to the fleet
GM1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
3

⚠️ Review de la risk management

▼
Review of the risk register
Risk register update, changes in risk levels, new risks identified and closed risks
ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
Effectiveness of safety barriers
Verification that the mitigation measures in place remain effective and appropriate for the identified risks
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
Risks related to organizational changes
Risk assessment of changes that occurred (new types, new routes, restructurings, new contracts)
ORO.GEN.200(a)(7)
Critical operational risks (Top 5)
Presentation of the 5 most critical operational risks, action status and validation by the accountable manager
GM1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(3)
4

👥 Organization, competencies & resources

▼
Review of key positions and nominations
Key positions filled, deputies in place and nominations notified to the competent authority
ORO.GEN.200(a)(1) / ORO.GEN.210
Adequacy of the resources humaines
Sufficient staffing for the activity volume, recruitment plan if necessary
ORO.GEN.210
SMS/CMS training review
SMS/CMS training coverage rate, training completed vs planned, Y+1 plan
ORO.GEN.200(a)(4)
Financial resources allocated to CMS/SMS
Budget dedicated to compliance and safety, adequacy with the besoins operational
ORO.GEN.200
Compliance Manager independence
Verification that the CM has direct access to the accountable manager and functional independence
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(1)
5

🔒 Safety policy & annual objectives

▼
Review and validation of the safety policy
Confirmation that the policy remains relevant, up to date, signed and communicated to all personnel
ORO.GEN.200(a)(2)
Review of Y-1 safety objectives
Assessment of the achievement rate for each objective set the previous year and analysis of deviations
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(2)
Definition of Y+1 safety objectives
Fixation of the new SMART objectives for l'following year, with indicateurs of mesure and responsables
ORO.GEN.200(a)(2)
Just Culture (Just Culture)
Assessment of l'application of the just culture, feedback on the reports, anonymization
ORO.GEN.200 / Reg. EU 376/2014
6

📅 Approval of Y+1 audit programme

▼
Presentation of the Y+1 audit programme
Audit schedule, covered domains, frequency, designated auditors, prioritization criteria
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
Full coverage of activities within ≤ 24 months
Verification that all regulated activities are covered in the audit cycle (12 to 24 months max.)
AMC2 ORO.GEN.200
Formal approval by the accountable manager
Signature of the Y+1 audit programme by the accountable manager during the SRB meeting
ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
Auditor resources verified
Qualified and independent auditors available for each audit planned, use of external auditors if necessary
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(4)
7

🏛 Regulatory interfaces & relations with the authority

▼
Report on contacts with the competent authority
Summary of exchanges with la DGAC/EASA : inspections, authority audits, regulatory letters, responses
ORO.GEN.130
Impactful regulatory developments
New regulations or amendments in force or upcoming, impact on operations, action plan
ORO.GEN.110
Status of open authority findings
Findings from DGAC/EASA audits, status of submitted CAPs, response deadlines met
ORO.GEN.130
Pending notifications and approvals
List of approval or notification requests soumises to the authority : MEL, OM, training programmes, etc.
ORO.MLR.101 / ORO.GEN.130
8

✅ Decisions & actions arising from the SRB

▼
Formal management decisions
Commitments made by the accountable manager during the meeting : allocated resources, priorities set, validated deadlines
ORO.GEN.200
Action plan arising from the SRB
List of actions decided in the SRB with designated owner, deadline and closure criterion for each action
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
Minutes signed by the accountable manager
The SRB report must be signed by the accountable manager and retained for ≥ 5 years, available to the authority
ORO.GEN.220 / AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
Communication of SRB results to personnel
Summary communicated to all relevant departments within 30 days after the SRB
GM1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
Next SRB date set
The next SRB is planned within 12 months. La date and the planned participants sont recorded during the meeting
AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)

Ph.2

June
Ph.4
SPO — HESLO & MCF operations · SPO.GEN · SPO.OP.150 · SPO.OP.230 · SPO.SPEC.HESLO · SPO.SPEC.MCF · SPO.FC.120 17 pts

November

December
Ph.8
Annual Safety Review Board + Management review · ORO.GEN.200 · CAMO.A.200(a)(3) · ICAO Annex 19 SRB

SPO.SPEC.HESLO.105(a)M.A.501(a)M.A.502(a)
Annual audit cycle — 8 phases
  • Jan–Fév
    Phase 1 — SMS Management system audit
    Safety policy, responsibilities, objectives, SPI/KPI, SMS documentation. Review of previous year annual report. MOC verification for the past year.
    ORO.GEN.200 · ORO.GEN.205 · ORO.GEN.210 · ORO.GEN.130 · ORO.MLR.100
  • Mars
    Phase 2 — CAT + NCC operational audit
    Normal and emergency procedures, MEL, fuel, minima, M&B, crew documentation, OM-A/B/C/D. CAT/NCC/IDE equipment.
    CAT.GEN · CAT.OP.MPA · CAT.IDE.H · NCC.GEN · NCC.OP · NCC.IDE.H · ORO.FC
  • Avr–Mai
    Phase 3 — SPA audit — EFB & Dangerous goods
    EFB compliance (hardware, software, procedures, backup), DG programme, crew training, IATA/ICAO documentation, DG emergency procedures.
    SPA.EFB.100 · SPA.DG.100 · SPA.DG.105
  • Juin
    Phase 4 — HESLO & MCF audit — Specialised operations
    SPO risk assessment, rescue plans, HESLO/MCF training, equipment, ground/air coordination. Site observation if possible.
    SPO.GEN · SPO.OP.150 · SPO.OP.200 · SPO.OP.230 · SPO.SPEC.HESLO · SPO.SPEC.MCF · SPO.FC.120
  • Juil–Août
    Phase 5 — Part-M / CAMO airworthiness audit
    AS 332 C1 maintenance programme, AD/SB tracking, LLP, CRS, TLB, CAMO/Part-145 interface, ARC in force. Physical inspection of one aircraft.
    M.A.201 à M.A.903 · CAMO.A.100 à CAMO.A.320 · M.A.708(c)
  • Sep–Oct
    Phase 6 — Crew qualifications & training audit
    Licences, AS 332 type ratings, HESLO/MCF competency, recurrencies, CRM/MCC records, individual training tracking.
    ORO.FC.005 · ORO.FC.100 à .145 · SPO.FC.120 · CAT.OP.MPA.175
  • Nov
    Phase 7 — Surveillance audit — Non-conformity closure
    Verification of finding closure from phases 1 to 6. Corrective actions, effectiveness verified, continuous improvement plan.
    ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) · CAMO.A.315 · M.A.712
  • Déc
    Phase 8 — Annual Safety Review Board + Revue de direction
    SPI/KPI summary (operations + airworthiness), occurrence analysis, safety policy review, N+1 objectives, crew communication.
    ORO.GEN.200 · CAMO.A.200(a)(3) · ICAO Annex 19
Audit plan performance KPIs
Plan coverage
% of domains audited vs planned for the year. Target: 100%
ORO.GEN.200(a)(3) · CAMO.A.315
Finding closure rate
% findings closed on time. Target: ≥ 85% within 60 days
ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) · M.A.712
L1 processing time
Average Level 1 closure time. Target: ≤ 7 working days
ORO.AOC.135(b) · CAMO.A.115
Aircraft availability
Available hours / scheduled hours AS 332 C1. Target ≥ 85%
CAMO.A.200(a)(3) · M.A.302
📋 Corrective Action Plan
Tracking of corrective actions for findings (deviations) raised during audits. Each entry records the audit, the finding level, the nominated person responsible, the corrective-action status and its effectiveness verification. Compliant with AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) (management of findings & corrective actions) and GM1 ORO.GEN.150 (closure deadlines).
⚠ EASA reference deadlines (GM1 ORO.GEN.150): Level 1 → corrective action within 30 days; Level 2 → accepted action plan, then closure within the agreed deadline (usually max. 90 days). A completed corrective action should be subject to effectiveness verification before the finding is considered closed.
Audit N°Audit titleLevelFinding description Nominated PersonDateCorrective actionDeadline StatusNotifiedEffectiveness
📁 Mission Orders, Audit Reports & Authority
Formal management of audit missions (opening, scope, auditor, approval), production of structured audit reports, and follow-up of contacts/commitments with the competent authority (FOCA/OFAC). Compliant with AMC2 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) and ORO.GEN.130/.135.
📝 Audit mission orders AMC2 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)
Mission ref.Scope / domainAuditor(s)Planned dateApproved by (CM)StatusReport
🏛 Follow-up of contacts & commitments — Competent authority (FOCA) ORO.GEN.130 / .135
ORO.GEN.130 : any change affecting the certificate/specifications must be notified and approved before implementation. ORO.GEN.135 : the operator gives the authority access and responds to its requests within the required deadlines.
DateTypeSubjectAuthority ref.Commitment / deadlineStatus
⚙ Settings — Indicator targets
Define the target values for each performance indicator (KPI/SPI) here. These thresholds drive the alerts (green / amber / red) on the Dashboard, the KPI — Compliance tab and the Register & CAP. Finding processing deadlines (Level 1 / Level 2) are also used to calculate deadline compliance. Default values correspond to usual EASA expectations.
🎯 Target definition
For each indicator : Target = objective to achieve (green if achieved) · Alert threshold = intermediate limit (orange between the two, red beyond) · Direction = « ≥ » if a higher value is better (compliance), « ≤ » if a lower value is better (findings, deadlines).
IndicatorDirectionTargetAlert threshold (orange)Unit

Corrective Action

✅ Effectiveness verification AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)

Ordre de mission d'audit

Report d'audit formel

Structured report compliant with AMC2 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6): identification, scope, audit conduct, summary of classified findings, conclusions, distribution and signatures. Findings entered here feed the central register.

Authority contact / commitment (FOCA)

🔗 List of Subcontractors
Monitoring of contracted organizations (maintenance, training, handling, expertise). Compliant with AMC2 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) & ORO.GEN.205 — continuous monitoring of provider compliance.
⚠ Requirements EASA : the operator remains responsible for contracted services (ORO.GEN.205). Each contractor must be audited periodically. Generated findings automatically feed the Register & CAP.
Ref.OrganizationService typeRegulatory reference Certificate / ApprovalApproval dateExpiry Audit frequencyLast auditNext audit StatusDocuments

Contractor record

📎 Attachments (contract, certificate, audit report)
Accepted formats : PDF, JPG, PNG — max 5 Mo par fichier. Files are stored in base64 in localStorage.
👤 Internal Auditors & Inspectors
Formal qualification, authorizations and recurrent training of CMS internal auditors. Compliant with AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6) — the auditor must be competent, trained and independent of the audited domain.
⚠ Independence principle (AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)) : the auditor must not audit their own work area. Recurrent training must be documented. Qualification is maintained as long as periodic training is up to date.
AuditorMayn functionInitial training Init. dateLast recurrent trainingNext recurrent training Authorized domainsExcluded domains (independence) No. of audits completedAuthorization status

Internal auditor record

🎓 List of Trainers
Register of internal and external trainers authorized to deliver training within the SAF International training programme. Compliant with ORO.GEN.200(a)(4) — competency, qualification and currency of instructors.
⚠ Trainer qualification must be kept current. A trainer must not deliver training in a domain where their own qualification has lapsed. Recurrent qualification must be documented.
TrainerMain functionTrainer type Qualification / certificateQual. dateLast recurrent trainingNext recurrent training Training domainsNo. of sessionsStatus

Trainer record

📊 Annual monitoring report — CMS
Annual summary report of the compliance management system, to be presented to the Accountable Manager and transferable to FOCA. Compliant with AMC1 ORO.GEN.200(a)(6)§e — formal periodic review requirement.
⚙ Report settings

🛟 Données Safety (SMS) — lecture seule

Risques (triés par gravité OACI) et occurrences issus du module SMS, en consultation pour l'audit. Pour modifier ces données, utilisez l'outil SMS.

Risques — du plus grave au moins grave

Occurrences

SAF International — CMS
